name: BookBot
tagline: Automates appointment scheduling and calendar management
description: BookBot handles end-to-end booking workflows — from parsing user intent to confirming appointments. It integrates with calendars, sends reminders, and handles rescheduling and cancellations gracefully.
use_cases: Schedule meetings across time zones without back-and-forth emails
Handle customer appointment booking for clinics or salons
Auto-reschedule when conflicts arise
example_prompt: Schedule a 30-minute call with John next Tuesday afternoon and send a calendar invite

name: FAQly
tagline: Answers customer questions from your knowledge base instantly
description: FAQly ingests your docs, PDFs, and help articles and responds to customer queries with accurate, cited answers. It escalates to humans when confidence is low.
use_cases: Reduce support ticket volume by 60%
Answer product questions 24/7 without human agents
Onboard new users with guided FAQ flows
example_prompt: What is your refund policy and how do I initiate a return?

name: TriageAI
tagline: Classifies and routes support tickets automatically
description: TriageAI reads incoming support tickets, determines urgency and category, assigns to the right team, and drafts an initial response. Cuts triage time from hours to seconds.
use_cases: Auto-route tickets to billing, technical, or general support queues
Flag high-priority or VIP issues for immediate human review
Draft personalized first-response emails
example_prompt: My payment failed but I was still charged twice. This is urgent.

name: ReportGen
tagline: Turns raw data into executive-ready reports
description: ReportGen connects to your data sources, runs analysis, and produces structured reports with insights, charts, and summaries. Supports scheduled delivery and custom templates.
use_cases: Generate weekly business performance summaries automatically
Create investor-ready reports from raw financial data
Build real-time dashboards from SQL query results
example_prompt: Generate a monthly sales report comparing Q1 vs Q2 with top performing SKUs

name: DataHarvest
tagline: Scrapes and structures web data at scale
description: DataHarvest navigates websites, extracts structured data, handles pagination and anti-bot measures, and delivers clean JSON or CSV output. Handles dynamic JavaScript-heavy pages.
use_cases: Monitor competitor pricing daily
Aggregate job listings across multiple boards
Extract product data from e-commerce sites
example_prompt: Scrape all product names, prices, and reviews from this e-commerce category page

name: Condense
tagline: Summarizes long documents, threads, and meetings
description: Condense takes any long-form content — PDFs, Slack threads, meeting transcripts, research papers — and returns crisp, structured summaries with key takeaways and action items.
use_cases: Summarize hour-long meeting recordings into bullet points
Extract key insights from 100-page research reports
Catch up on long Slack or email threads in seconds
example_prompt: Summarize this 40-page investor report and highlight the 5 most important points

name: DraftCraft
tagline: Writes on-brand content from a brief
description: DraftCraft learns your brand voice, tone guidelines, and target audience to produce blog posts, landing pages, product descriptions, and more. Supports SEO optimization and human-in-the-loop review.
use_cases: Produce 10 SEO blog posts per week from keyword briefs
Write product descriptions for entire catalog
Generate landing page copy from a value proposition
example_prompt: Write a 1000-word SEO blog post about the benefits of async work for remote teams

name: PostPilot
tagline: Plans and publishes your social media content
description: PostPilot takes a content brief or blog post and repurposes it into platform-native posts for Twitter/X, LinkedIn, and Instagram. Schedules optimal posting times and tracks engagement.
use_cases: Repurpose one blog post into a week of social content
Maintain consistent posting schedule without manual effort
A/B test post variations automatically
example_prompt: Turn this blog post into 5 tweets, 2 LinkedIn posts, and 3 Instagram captions

name: InboxZero
tagline: Drafts, sorts, and responds to emails intelligently
description: InboxZero reads your inbox, categorizes emails by urgency, drafts contextual replies in your voice, and handles routine correspondence autonomously with your approval.
use_cases: Draft replies to 50 similar customer emails at once
Auto-respond to common inquiry types
Summarize and prioritize inbox every morning
example_prompt: Draft a polite follow-up email to a client who hasn't responded in 5 days

name: ReviewBot
tagline: Reviews PRs for bugs, style, and security issues
description: ReviewBot analyzes pull requests, flags potential bugs, security vulnerabilities, and style violations, and leaves inline comments. Integrates with GitHub and GitLab CI/CD pipelines.
use_cases: Catch security vulnerabilities before they reach production
Enforce coding standards across a large team
Speed up code review cycles for solo devs
example_prompt: Review this PR for SQL injection vulnerabilities and performance issues

name: BugSorter
tagline: Triages and prioritizes bug reports automatically
description: BugSorter reads incoming bug reports, reproduces steps, assesses severity, links to related issues, and assigns to the appropriate engineer. Reduces bug backlog chaos.
use_cases: Auto-label and assign bugs from user reports
Detect duplicate issues before they pile up
Estimate fix complexity for sprint planning
example_prompt: Triage this crash report and find similar existing issues in our backlog

name: PRDigest
tagline: Generates human-readable PR summaries for teams
description: PRDigest analyzes code diffs and commit messages to produce clear, non-technical summaries for stakeholders, changelogs, and release notes. Saves engineering time on documentation.
use_cases: Auto-generate release notes from merged PRs
Keep non-technical stakeholders informed on progress
Produce weekly engineering digests
example_prompt: Summarize what changed in this sprint's merged PRs for a non-technical audience

name: InvoiceFlow
description: InvoiceFlow extracts data from invoice PDFs, matches them to purchase orders, flags discrepancies, and pushes approved invoices to your accounting system. Handles high volumes without manual entry.
use_cases: Process 500 vendor invoices per month without manual entry
Flag invoices that don't match PO amounts
Auto-approve recurring invoices from trusted vendors
example_prompt: Extract line items from this invoice PDF and match it against our purchase order

name: LeadLens
tagline: Qualifies and scores inbound leads instantly
description: LeadLens analyzes lead form submissions, enriches data from public sources, scores leads by fit and intent, and routes hot leads to sales reps immediately. Reduces time wasted on poor-fit prospects.
use_cases: Score and rank 200 weekly inbound leads automatically
Enrich leads with company size, funding, and tech stack
Auto-book demos for leads above a quality threshold
example_prompt: Score this lead and determine if they're a good fit for our enterprise plan

name: MeetMate
description: MeetMate handles back-and-forth scheduling for sales calls, interviews, and team syncs. It reads availability, proposes times, sends invites, and handles rescheduling — all via email or chat.
use_cases: Coordinate interviews across 5 interviewers and a candidate
Schedule sales demos without SDR involvement
Run automated scheduling for a coaching or consulting business
example_prompt: Find a time for a 45-minute interview with 3 interviewers and the candidate next week
